evidence: README structure diagram shows leapfrogai_api/ but actual directories are cowabunga_api/
backend/composer.py imports chat_complete and chat_complete_stream_raw from routers/openai/chat.py
backend/composer.py imports Session from routers/supabase_session.py
typedef/runs/run_create_base.py imports Session from routers/supabase_session.py
typedef/threads/thread_create.py imports Session from routers/supabase_session.py
typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py imports Session from routers/supabase_session.py
Model backends (vllm, whisper, repeater, text-embeddings, llama-cpp-python) have source in packages/ not src/
Both routers/openai/vector_stores.py and routers/cowabunga/vector_stores.py exist with overlapping concerns
Both routers/openai/models.py and routers/cowabunga/models.py exist with unclear separation
cowabunga_sdk/__init__.py executes print() at module import time
high_level_elegance.issues_preventing_higher_score: Layering violations where backend and typedef modules import from routers; documentation drift from fork rename showing stale paths; backend source split between src/ and packages/; overlapping openai vs cowabunga router surfaces blur endpoint ownership

high_level_elegance.dimension_character: The codebase has a sensible three-package decomposition (API, evals, SDK) and domain-aligned OpenAI router organization, but layering violations, stale documentation from a fork rename, and a split backend code layout between src/ and packages/ blur ownership and undermine predictability.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The top-level package structure maps well to capabilities and the typedef/CRUD organization follows OpenAI domain boundaries, but backend modules importing routers, type definitions importing session infrastructure, and README paths that diverge from runtime reality mean a new engineer cannot fully trust the documented architecture or predict change boundaries.

issues.summary: README and DEVELOPMENT.md reference stale leapfrogai_* paths after repository rename
evidence: README.md structure diagram shows src/leapfrogai_api/, src/leapfrogai_evals/, src/leapfrogai_sdk/ but actual directories are src/cowabunga_api/, src/cowabunga_evals/, src/cowabunga_sdk/
DEVELOPMENT.md references leapfrogai_api/config.example.yaml and leapfrogai-api package names that do not match current directory names
issues.suggestion: Update README.md and DEVELOPMENT.md to reference the current cowabunga_* directory names and package names, or add a prominent note about the rename if the old names are still used in deployment artifacts

issues.summary: Backend composer imports from router layer, violating expected dependency direction
evidence: backend/composer.py line 37: from cowabunga_api.routers.openai.chat import chat_complete, chat_complete_stream_raw
backend/composer.py line 38: from cowabunga_api.routers.supabase_session import Session
issues.suggestion: Extract the chat completion logic from routers/openai/chat.py into backend/chat_service.py or similar, and move Session/auth dependencies to a shared middleware/dependencies module that both backend and routers can import without creating a cycle

issues.summary: Type definition modules import Session from router layer, coupling types to transport
evidence: typedef/runs/run_create_base.py line 33: from cowabunga_api.routers.supabase_session import Session
typedef/threads/thread_create.py line 14: from cowabunga_api.routers.supabase_session import Session
typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py line 20: from cowabunga_api.routers.supabase_session import Session
issues.suggestion: Move the Session type and its creation logic to a neutral location such as src/cowabunga_api/data/database/session.py or src/cowabunga_api/dependencies/session.py so that typedef and backend modules can depend on it without referencing routers

issues.summary: Model backend implementations live in packages/ rather than under src/, creating disjoint code universes
evidence: packages/vllm/src/main.py is a 265-line model backend that imports from cowabunga_sdk but lives outside src/
packages/repeater/main.py is an 88-line backend entry point outside src/
packages/whisper/main.py, packages/text-embeddings/main.py, and packages/llama-cpp-python/main.py are similarly standalone
src/cowabunga_sdk/ exists but does not contain backend implementations; it only contains the SDK framework and protobuf stubs
issues.suggestion: Either consolidate backend implementations under src/cowabunga_backends/ (or similar) alongside the SDK they consume, or document clearly in README and package-level READMEs that packages/ contains both deployment artifacts and backend source code, and explain why this split exists

issues.summary: Duplicate endpoint domains exist in both routers/openai/ and routers/cowabunga/ with unclear ownership
evidence: routers/openai/vector_stores.py defines OpenAI-compatible vector store endpoints
routers/cowabunga/vector_stores.py defines custom Cowabunga vector store endpoints
routers/openai/models.py defines OpenAI-compatible model listing
routers/cowabunga/models.py defines custom Cowabunga model endpoints
No documentation explains when to add to openai/ vs cowabunga/ or whether cowabunga/ endpoints are extensions or alternatives
issues.suggestion: Document the ownership boundary in a routers/README.md: openai/ is for OpenAI-compatible API surface, cowabunga/ is for custom extensions. Consider merging or namespacing overlapping domains (e.g., cowabunga/v1/models vs openai/v1/models) so a single domain concept does not have two homes

issues.summary: SDK public surface triggers print() on every import
evidence: src/cowabunga_sdk/__init__.py line 71: print('Initializing CowabungaAI')
This executes whenever any consumer imports from cowabunga_sdk, including backend services and the API
issues.suggestion: Remove the print statement or replace it with a logging call guarded by a logger.isEnabledFor check; a library package should not produce stdout on import

root_causes: Fork rename (leapfrogai -> cowabunga) left documentation and some artifacts out of sync with runtime reality
FastAPI router modules accumulated shared dependencies (Session, auth) that were not extracted to a neutral layer, causing backend and typedef to import upward from routers
Monorepo layout conflates deployment packages (packages/) with backend source code, likely because backends are deployed as independent microservices
likely_symptoms: orphaned_file flags on packages/ backend entry points are false positives due to CLI/dynamic usage, but they reveal structural separation
signature variance across create_assistant, create_vector_store, create_thread, etc. may stem from openai vs cowabunga duplications

add.description: src/ is cleanly divided into cowabunga_api (API server), cowabunga_evals (evaluation framework), and cowabunga_sdk (model backend SDK). Each maps to a distinct capability and domain, making high-level navigation straightforward.

add.header: OpenAI domain-aligned router and typedef organization
add.description: routers/openai/ and typedef/ are organized by OpenAI API entity (assistants, chat, completions, embeddings, files, messages, runs, threads, vector_stores). This makes the API surface predictable and mirrors the external contract.
data/database/ provides a clean factory and abstract base for both Supabase and Turso backends, localizing database backend choice to a single configuration point without leaking into CRUD logic.

add.header: Stale README paths after fork rename
add.description: The README and DEVELOPMENT.md still reference leapfrogai_api/, leapfrogai_evals/, and leapfrogai_sdk/ paths, but the actual directories were renamed to cowabunga_*. This documentation drift undermines trust for new engineers.

add.header: Backend and typedef modules depend on router layer
add.description: backend/composer.py imports from routers/openai/chat.py, and multiple typedef/ modules import Session from routers/supabase_session.py. These cross-layer dependencies violate the expected directionality where routers depend on backend and types, not vice versa.

add.header: Backend source split between src/ and packages/
add.description: Model backend implementations (vllm, whisper, repeater, text-embeddings, llama-cpp-python) live as standalone Python files under packages/ rather than under src/cowabunga_sdk/ or src/cowabunga_api/. This creates two disconnected code universes and makes backend logic hard to discover.
